signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.07 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains bid; Middle East tensions introduce intervention risk on a break above 160.00. JPY COT LF short −61,340 (crowded carry). Carry dominates until VIX > 25. Watch for the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) to determine the yield gap direction.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.876 — AUD 1W avg: +0.432% (n=4) outperforms JPY 1W avg: -0.457% (n=7) with a G8 spread: 0.888%. 360bp RBA-BoJ carry favors AUD. Watch for AUD/JPY to extend gains on strong Australian performance and JPY weakness.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7252 — AUD COT LF net long +58,994, WoW delta, and retail 15% long / 85% short. Market hedging upside rather than chasing — late-trend caution, not reversal. Watch for the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) to determine the interest rate direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1784 — EUR COT LF net short -21,011. Market volatility contained with HV30 at 8.2%. Watch for the next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) to determine the interest rate direction.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7778 — retail sentiment 85% long / 15% short. Contrarian reversal risk due to extreme long positioning. Watch for a potential reversal in the pair.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5958, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -19.3k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ bias points to a hike with 40% priced in. This creates an asymmetric risk profile as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling, likely to lead to a sharp unwind if the central bank delivers on its forward bias. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 27 May will be a key catalyst for this trade.
